(SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR MR. PYLE IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH THE CORRECT TITLE OF THEIR MOTION. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chris Baker, Esq. 415-433-1064 of Baker Curtis & Schwartz for Plaintiff Titilayo Ala.
Kyle Schriner, Esq. 415-321-4924 of Schriner Law Firm for Defendant RICHARD PYLE.
Theodora Lee, Esq. 925-932-2468 of Littler Mendelson for Defendant CH2M HILL COMPANIES LTD., CH2M HILL, INC., CH2M HILL ENGINEERS, INC. through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views on gender violence, defamation, reads case law. Plaintiff argues legislative history is not helpful, new issue of law. Defendant CH2M argues defamation, malice. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CH2M HILL COMPANIES LTD., CH2M HILL, INC., CH2M HILL ENGINEERS, INC. DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The tentative ruling is adopted. Defendants CH2M Hill Companies, Ltd., CH2M Hill, Inc., and CH2M Hill Engineers, Inc.'s demurrer to the third, seventh and eleventh causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Titilayo Ala is: a) sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for gender violence; and b) overruled as to the seventh and eleventh causes of action for defamation and violation of the Unfair Competition Law. As an employer, per Civil Code 53.4(e), the CH2M entities are not liable for statutory gender violence because there is no allegation that they personally committed the violence. Federal courts are in accord with this understanding of the statute and Ms. Ala relies on inapposite authorities. The defamation claim is adequately alleged. Ms. Ala alleges that both defendant Richard Pyle and individuals on the Employee Relations Team made defamatory statements, yet the CH2M entities focus only on the defamation that the Employee Relations Team allegedly committed. Liberally construing the FAC, Ms. Ala sufficiently alleges that the CH2M entities are vicariously liable for Mr. Pyle's alleged defamatory conduct that occurred in the course and scope of his employment at CH2M. The FAC does not disclose on its face that the alleged defamatory statements are within the scope of the conditional privilege. Moreover, the FAC adequately alleges that the alleged defamatory statements were made maliciously based on the allegation that Mr. Pyle made up the statements. The FAC also alleges that the Employee Relations Team did not credit Mr. Pyle's statements but repeated them anyway. Ms. Ala sufficiently alleges economic injury to support her UCL claim. (Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 163, 177-178; Kwikset Corp. v. Sup. Crt. (2011) 51 Cal.4th 310, 323-325.) (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chris Baker, Esq. 415-433-1064 of Baker Curtis & Schwartz for Plaintiff Titilayo Ala.
Kyle Schriner, Esq. 415-321-4924 of Schriner Law Firm for Defendant RICHARD PYLE.
Theodora Lee, Esq. 925-932-2468 of Littler Mendelson for Defendant CH2M HILL COMPANIES LTD., CH2M HILL, INC., CH2M HILL ENGINEERS, INC. through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views on gender violence, defamation, reads case law. Plaintiff argues legislative history is not helpful, new issue of law. Defendant CH2M argues defamation, malice. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT RICHARD PYLE'S JOINDER TO DEFENDANT CH2M'S DEMURRER TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION SET FORTH IN PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Richard Pyle's joinder in the demurrer of the CH2M entities to the third, seventh and eleventh causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Titilayo Ala is overruled as to all three causes of action. The first amended complaint adequately alleges that Mr. Pyle personally committed gender violence against Ms. Ala. Mr. Pyle's demurrer to the defamation and UCL claims are overruled for the reasons explained in the ruling on the demurrer filed by the CH2M entities.

Counsel for Mr. Pyle is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with the correct title of their motion.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
